title: Migrate CI to Argo Workflows
description: Replace any existing GitHub Actions CI with an Argo Workflows WorkflowTemplate in iad-ci. Template should: go fmt check, go vet, go build. ccdash is a Go project. Store template in jedarden/declarative-config under k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/ccdash-ci.yaml. Follow patterns from existing WorkflowTemplates in that repo.

title: Multi-directory JSONL scanning
description: Allow ccdash to aggregate token usage from JSONL files across multiple directories rather than a single path. Configurable via CLI flag or config file. Should support glob patterns or a list of directories. Useful for users with multiple Claude Code project roots.

title: Publish ccdash binary as GitHub Release via Argo CI
description: On successful CI pass, have Argo Workflows publish the ccdash binary as a GitHub Release artifact. Tag-triggered. Build multi-arch binaries (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64). Follow the pattern from forge-ci or needle-ci WorkflowTemplates in jedarden/declarative-config. Include SHA256 checksums.

title: Network I/O monitoring panel
description: Add a network I/O panel to ccdash showing bandwidth usage per process (bytes sent/received per second), complementing the existing CPU/memory panels. Use /proc/net/dev or per-process /proc/<pid>/net/dev on Linux. Display as a scrollable table similar to existing panels.
